skill.name: python-scripting
skill.description: Python scripting with uv and PEP 723 inline dependencies. Use when creating standalone Python scripts with automatic dependency management.
skill.summary: Create standalone Python scripts with uv and PEP 723 inline dependency metadata.
skill.author: Joseph OBrien

security_audit.summary: Static weak cryptography detections are false positives; the cited lines contain front matter, docstring placeholders, and argparse formatter settings, not cryptographic algorithms. Static Ruby or shell backtick detections are Markdown code fences and inline command examples, not executable Ruby backticks. The real concern is medium risk because generated uv scripts can run local code and automatically install declared dependencies.

medium_findings.title: Automatic Dependency Installation Through uv Scripts
medium_findings.description: The skill creates scripts intended to run through uv and notes that dependencies install automatically on first run. This is a legitimate Python workflow, but it can fetch and execute third-party packages selected during script generation, so users should review dependencies before running generated scripts.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited files explicitly show uv script execution and automatic dependency installation. The behavior is legitimate, but it creates supply-chain and execution risk when dependency choices are untrusted.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL Is Documentation Reference
low_findings.description: The hardcoded URL points to uv documentation in a requirements section. No code sends data to this URL or performs a network request from the template.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL appears inside a docstring as a requirement reference. There is no HTTP client call or data transmission associated with it.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Detections Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The cited lines do not contain cryptographic algorithms. They contain YAML description text, a template docstring placeholder, an argparse description field, an argparse formatter class, and a best practice about exit codes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual inspection found no MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar weak cryptographic usage on the cited lines. The matches appear to be scanner pattern errors.

low_findings.title: Backtick Execution Detections Are Markdown Formatting
low_findings.description: The cited SKILL.md lines use Markdown code fences and inline code to document a template path and uv commands. They do not execute Ruby backticks or shell commands by themselves.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The backticks are Markdown delimiters around documentation examples. There is no Ruby file, command substitution, or runtime evaluation in these lines.

dangerous_patterns.title: uv Script Execution With Declared Dependencies
dangerous_patterns.description: The template uses a uv script shebang and PEP 723 dependency metadata. This pattern is useful, but generated scripts should not be run until dependencies and script logic are reviewed.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The uv execution pattern and dependency metadata are directly present. The risk depends on the generated dependencies and code, so confidence is high for the pattern and moderate for exploitability.

content.user_title: Create Portable Python Scripts with uv
content.value_statement: Standalone Python utilities often need repeatable dependencies and clear command-line behavior. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code draft uv-based scripts with PEP 723 metadata.
actual_capabilities: Creates standalone Python script plans using uv and PEP 723 metadata.
Defines inline Python version and dependency requirements for generated scripts.
Sets up argparse-style command-line inputs, output paths, and verbose flags.
Encourages reproducible dependency resolution with an exclude-newer date.
Provides a reusable template for focused utility scripts and data tasks.
limitations: It does not audit third-party Python packages for safety.
It does not guarantee that generated scripts are production ready.
It assumes uv and Python 3.12 or newer are available.
It provides a template, not a complete application framework.

use_cases.title: Build one-off automation scripts
use_cases.description: Create a focused Python utility with arguments, dependency metadata, and clear usage notes.

use_cases.title: Process local data files
use_cases.description: Draft a script that accepts an input path, writes an output file, and reports progress when verbose mode is enabled.

use_cases.title: Prototype internal CLI tools
use_cases.description: Start a small command-line tool with repeatable dependencies before promoting it into a larger package.

prompt_templates.title: Create a simple utility script
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a standalone Python script using this skill. It should read one input file, write one output file, and include help text.

prompt_templates.title: Add managed dependencies
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a uv Python script for my task. Include PEP 723 metadata, list only necessary dependencies, and explain why each dependency is needed.

prompt_templates.title: Design a data processing CLI
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a Python CLI script that validates arguments, supports verbose output, handles missing files, and writes a clear result summary.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a reproducible automation script
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production-oriented uv script with pinned dependency timing, structured error handling, documented exit codes, and safe file handling.

output_examples.input: Create a script that summarizes a CSV file.
output_examples.output: A standalone uv script outline with dependency metadata, file path arguments, validation steps, and a summary report format.

output_examples.input: Turn my manual log cleanup task into a CLI.
output_examples.output: A focused command-line utility plan with input selection, dry-run behavior, verbose reporting, and clear success or failure exit codes.

output_examples.input: Make a repeatable script for API result processing.
output_examples.output: A script design that declares required packages, separates configuration from processing, and documents how to run it with uv.

best_practices: Review every generated dependency before running the script.
Keep each script focused on one clear task.
Use explicit argument validation and documented exit codes.
anti_patterns: Do not run generated scripts before reviewing their dependencies and file operations.
Do not include broad dependency lists when the standard library is enough.
Do not store secrets directly inside generated script files.

faq.question: What does this skill create?
faq.answer: It helps create standalone Python scripts that use uv and PEP 723 inline dependency metadata.

faq.question: Does this skill require uv?
faq.answer: Yes. The template is designed for scripts run with uv and Python 3.12 or newer.

faq.question: Can it create command-line tools?
faq.answer: Yes. It guides argument parsing, input paths, output paths, verbose mode, and exit code behavior.

faq.question: Does it install dependencies automatically?
faq.answer: Scripts run through uv can install declared dependencies on first run. Review dependencies before execution.

faq.question: Is it safe for sensitive data workflows?
faq.answer: Use caution. Review generated file handling, logging, dependencies, and secret handling before processing sensitive data.

faq.question: Can Claude, Codex, and Claude Code use it?
faq.answer: Yes. The report lists support for Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
